# 📋 Business Rules — SalesFlow

> Every formula, constraint, and business logic rule the system must enforce.

---

## 1. Employee Rules

### Department Field Behavior
```
If department = "Sales":
  → Show: jobTitle dropdown
  → Show: seniorityLevel dropdown
  → Show: target (number input)
  → Show: currentTeam dropdown
  → Show: teamJoinDate

If department ≠ "Sales":
  → Hide all of the above fields
  → Do NOT save those fields to DB
```

### Seniority Levels (Sales Only)
```
Fresh → BA → BC → Senior → SV → TeamLeader → SalesManager
```

### Auto-Generated Employee Code
```
Format: EMP-XXXX (zero-padded 4 digits)
Examples: EMP-0001, EMP-0042, EMP-0150
Generated by backend on POST /employees
Uses atomic counter or max+1 from DB
```

### Working Days Calculation
```
Total Working Days:
  = TODAY - hireDate  (or endDate if employee left)

Quarter Working Days (for target calculation):
  For each team history record in the quarter:
    days = MIN(quarterEnd, leaveDate or today) - MAX(quarterStart, joinDate)
  Sum all days across all team records in that quarter
```

### Employee Status
```
isActive = true   → currently employed
isActive = false  → resigned / terminated (soft delete)
endDate is set    → date of leaving
```

---

## 2. Team Rules

### Team Name Rule
```
team.name = teamLeader.name   (auto-set when creating or updating leader)
If team leader changes → team name updates automatically
```

### Solo Employee Rule
```
An employee who works alone (no team) can be set as:
  teamLeaderId = their own employeeId
  memberIds = []
This allows target tracking without requiring a team
```

### Leader Resignation Flow
```
When team leader's isActive → false:
1. Team is flagged as leaderless
2. Admin must reassign all members to other active teams
3. Each member gets a new history record with new joinDate
4. Old team is soft-deleted after reassignment
```

### One Team at a Time
```
An employee can only be in ONE team at any given moment
employee.currentTeamId = the current team
History table tracks all previous teams
```

---

## 3. Quarter Rules

### Valid Quarters ONLY
```
Q1: months 1, 2, 3    (Jan 1 → Mar 31)
Q2: months 4, 5, 6    (Apr 1 → Jun 30)
Q3: months 7, 8, 9    (Jul 1 → Sep 30)
Q4: months 10, 11, 12 (Oct 1 → Dec 31)

Quarter ID format: "Q2-2026"

Quarter boundaries are FIXED calendar quarters.
Custom date ranges are NOT supported.
```

### Quarter Detection Function
```javascript
function getQuarterId(date) {
  const month = date.getMonth() + 1; // 1-12
  const year = date.getFullYear();
  const q = Math.ceil(month / 3);    // 1, 2, 3, or 4
  return `Q${q}-${year}`;
}

function getQuarterBounds(quarterId) {
  const [q, year] = quarterId.split('-');
  const quarterNum = parseInt(q.replace('Q', ''));
  const startMonth = (quarterNum - 1) * 3 + 1;
  const endMonth = quarterNum * 3;
  return {
    start: new Date(year, startMonth - 1, 1),
    end: new Date(year, endMonth, 0)  // last day of end month
  };
}
```

---

## 4. Target Rules

### Employee Adjusted Target
```
Full Target = employee.target (manual input, in EGP)
Quarter Days = 90 (fixed, always 90 regardless of actual calendar days)

Adjusted Target = (Full Target ÷ 90) × actualWorkingDaysInQuarter

actualWorkingDaysInQuarter = sum of working days across all team records
                              for this employee within this quarter
```

### Target Achievement
```
Achieved = SUM of commissionValue for all sales in the quarter
           where this employee is listed as a seller

Achievement % = (Achieved ÷ Adjusted Target) × 100
Gap = Adjusted Target - Achieved  (negative means exceeded target)
```

### Team Leader Target
```
Team Leader Adjusted Target = SUM of all active members' Adjusted Targets
                               in the same quarter
Note: Includes the TL's own sales target if they also sell
```

### Sales Manager Target
```
Sales Manager Target = SUM of all Team Leaders' Adjusted Targets
                       under this Sales Manager
```

---

## 5. Sales Rules

### Sale Number Auto-Generation
```
Format: SALE-XXXX (zero-padded 4 digits)
Examples: SALE-0001, SALE-0099, SALE-1000
Generated by backend on POST /sales
```

### Private Source Rule
```
If sale.source = "Private":
  → clientRegistrationDate field is HIDDEN and NOT required
  → isPrivateSource = true in DB

If sale.source ≠ "Private":
  → clientRegistrationDate is shown and required
```

### Developer Collection Percentage Options
```
Default dropdown options:
  - 100%    → factor = 1.0
  - 50%     → factor = 0.5
  - 33.333% → factor = 0.33333
  - 25%     → factor = 0.25

Manual input also allowed (any percentage value)
```

### Commission Calculation Chain (in order)
```javascript
// Step 1: Collected commission percentage
collectedCommissionPercentage = contractCommissionPercentage × (developerCollectionPercentage / 100)

// Step 2: Gross commission (includes VAT)
grossCommissionWithVAT = (collectedCommissionPercentage / 100) × unitValue

// Step 3: Net revenue (extract VAT)
netRevenue = grossCommissionWithVAT / 1.14

// Step 4: VAT amount
vat = netRevenue × 0.14

// Step 5: Withholding tax
withholdingTax = netRevenue × 0.05

// Step 6: Invoice amount
invoiceAmount = netRevenue + vat - withholdingTax
// Simplified: invoiceAmount = grossCommissionWithVAT - withholdingTax
```

### Seller Split Rules
```
- Minimum sellers: 1
- Maximum sellers: 4
- Each seller has a sharePercentage (number)
- SUM of all sharePercentages MUST equal exactly 100
- System blocks sale confirmation if sum ≠ 100

Per seller commission:
  commissionValue = (sharePercentage / 100) × grossCommissionWithVAT
```

### Sale Status Flow
```
draft → confirmed → claimed → collected

draft:     just created, can still edit freely
confirmed: locked, sellers sum = 100%, triggers quarter achievement update
claimed:   a claim has been created against this sale
collected: claim has been marked as collected
```

### Quarter Assignment
```
sale.quarterId = getQuarterId(sale.contractDate)
This determines which quarter the sale counts toward for target tracking
```

---

## 6. Claims Rules

### Claim Number Auto-Generation
```
Format: CLM-XXXX
Examples: CLM-0001, CLM-0050
```

### Claim Creation Rules
```
- Only confirmed or higher status sales can have claims
- One claim per sale maximum
- Claim inherits all financial data from the sale
```

### Claim Status Flow
```
pending → submitted → collected
                    → disputed (can go back to submitted)
```

### Actual Collection
```
When marking as collected:
  - collectionDate: required (actual date money received)
  - collectedAmount: required (may differ from expected)
  - If collectedAmount < commissionDue: flag as partial
```

---

## 7. Settings / Lookup Tables Rules

### Configurable Items
```
1. Sale Sources:
   - System defaults: "Private", "Website", "Referral", "Walk-in"
   - Admin can add / edit / delete (except defaults)
   - "Private" is special — cannot be deleted, triggers special behavior

2. Invoice Types:
   - System defaults: 
     "Standard Tax Invoice"
     "Standard Non-Tax Invoice"
     "Electronic Tax Invoice"
   - Admin can add more

3. Developer Collection Percentages:
   - System defaults: 100, 50, 33.333, 25
   - Manual input always available as fallback
```

### Soft Delete Rule for Settings
```
isDefault = true  → cannot be deleted (only edited)
isDefault = false → can be deleted
Deleting a setting that is used in existing sales → mark inactive only, never remove
```

---

## 8. Client (Developer) Rules

### Client Code Auto-Generation
```
Format: CLT-XXXX
Examples: CLT-0001, CLT-0020
```

### Client in Sale
```
When selecting a client in a sale:
  - Search by name or CLT code
  - clientName is denormalized into the sale document for display
  - Client info changes do NOT retroactively update old sales
```

---

## 9. Rounding Rules
```
All monetary values: round to 2 decimal places
All percentages: store as decimal (e.g. 2.5 for 2.5%)
Working days: integer (no fractional days)
Achievement percentage: round to 1 decimal place
```
